title: veronica spicata var. elatior
intro: Veronica spicata var. elatior, also known as tall speedwell, is a perennial plant species belonging to the plantain family (Plantaginaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits associated with Veronica spicata var. elatior include its potential to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of the plant are often used to make infusions, tinctures, and teas, which can be consumed to harness the plant's medicinal properties.
